response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior engineer with deep open-source involvement, Linux experience, and broad full-stack skills, but the role is an executive-level Head of Development position requiring proven leadership of teams and client-facing architecture consulting, which his track record only partially demonstrates (founder of a small open-source project, workshops, but no formal team-lead or management roles). Critically, the role requires German at minimum C1, and his resume lists German as basic — a hard disqualifier for a client-advisory position in this market.
strengths: Extensive hands-on software development experience across web, embedded, and high-performance systems
Genuine open-source commitment (GISCollective founder, D-language libraries, FOSS4G speaker) aligned with digital-sovereignty focus
Strong Linux, testing/TDD, and DevOps background with teaching and mentoring experience
concerns: German proficiency listed as basic, far below the required C1 minimum
No formal experience leading teams or holding operational/executive leadership roles
Limited evidence of UML, requirements management, or IT security/compliance advisory work

key_factors.factor: Lack of formal leadership/management track record
Strong open-source and Linux technical background
